title: ZXPong Overview
description: A practical introduction to ZXPong, the core paddle-and-ball loop, release-facing modes, and ZX Series support systems.
summary: ZXPong is a retro-inspired paddle arcade game in the ZX Series with Solo play, World PVP, skins, coins, achievements, and Game Center support.
heroImageAlt: ZXPong retro paddle game hero artwork

sections.heading: What is ZXPong?
paragraphs: ZXPong is part of the [ZX Series](/zx-series), focused on classic paddle-and-ball rallies, short sessions, and score improvement.

The current release-facing feature set includes Solo play, World PVP, AI level selection, unlockable paddle skins, collectible court coins, Game Center achievements, leaderboards, and BarkinMad Coins support.

sections.heading: Where to start
paragraphs: Start with How to Play, then read Controls and Game Modes before moving into coins, achievements, leaderboards, and strategy.
Use the guide navigation at the top of each page to move through the standard Website Framework v1.1 structure.

sections.heading: Release notes
paragraphs: ZXPong is live on the App Store.
Local PVP exists in retained app code but is not shown in the current release-facing menu. World PVP is the visible multiplayer route documented here.

featureShowcases.heading: ZXPong at a glance
featureShowcases.summary: Overview introduces ZXPong as a ZX Series pong arcade game.
body: This screen matters because it gives players the quickest route from discovering the game to understanding how it plays. ZXPong is presented around pong matches, so players can see the core loop before reading deeper guides.
Players use this page to decide where to go next: setup, controls, game modes, progression, screenshots, or support topics.
bullets: Links the app landing route to the documentation set.
Keeps public wording focused on player experience and supported features.

image.caption: ZXPong gameplay artwork introduces the app and its core loop.

featureShowcases.heading: Progression and support surfaces
featureShowcases.summary: ZXPong documentation connects gameplay with leaderboards, achievements, BarkinMad Coins, tips, FAQ, and screenshots.
body: This matters because ZX Series pages are not only image galleries. They should explain how the player moves from learning the game into repeated play and progress tracking.
Players use these supporting pages after the first session to understand goals, scoring surfaces, unlocks, and practical tips.
bullets: Connects gameplay to progression and support pages.
Keeps route structure consistent across the ZX Series.
Uses existing artwork as placeholders where page-specific screenshots are not available.

image.caption: ZXPong supporting artwork is used for progression and documentation context.
